content:
# Well Log Analysis

**Summary**: The analysis of geophysical logs to determine lithology and petrophysical properties.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] applied well-log-analysis including gamma-ray spectrometry to characterize the Sarah Formation.

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] used resistivity logging and interpretation to determine aquifer depth and salinity.

- [[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996]] used well-log_analysis to extract porosity values from 44 wells in the Harmaliyah Field.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used well-log-analysis for identifying lithostratigraphic units and predicting reservoir porosity.

- [[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003]] used well-log-analysis to derive porosity values for 19 wells in the Arab-D reservoir.

- [[2001-al-subai|Al-Subai, 2001]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate the consistency of data from five different wells.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] used well-log-analysis to establish and correlate major stratigraphic divisions in the Thamama Group.

- [[2009-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2009]] applied well-log-analysis to evaluate the impact of borehole wash-out and sliding on LWD data.

- [[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009]] used well-log-analysis to provide input data for neural network training.

- [[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010]] applied well-log-analysis to correlate core data with log responses for petrophysical rock identification.

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] used well-log-analysis to correlate core descriptions with gamma-ray logs for parasequence identification.

- [[2011-almubarak|ALMUBARAK, 2011]] used nodal analysis techniques to model reservoir inflow and well outflow performance for multi-lateral wells.

- [[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011]] applied well-log-analysis to extract mobility data across various depths.

- [[2011-al-dossary|Al-Dossary, 2011]] used GR logs to confirm cross-flow between the base of Arab-C and Arab-D.

- [[2011-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2011]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate casing points and identify formation boundaries like Formation-3 and Formation-15.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] used well-log-analysis to calibrate core data and construct 2D sequence stratigraphic cross sections.

- [[2011-al-nasser|Al-Nasser, 2011]] used well-log-analysis to calculate water saturation across Reservoir D and F using hydraulic unit parameters.

- [[2011-alburaikan|AlBuraikan, 2011]] used well-log-analysis for the initial characterization of well X-1 in the Unayzah formation.

- [[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012]] applied well-log-analysis using FMI tools to detect fracture types in the Ruman wells.

- [[2012-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2012]] used well-log-analysis to extract static properties like porosity and saturation for AI model input.

- [[2013-al-kharraa|Al-Kharraa, 2013]] applied well-log-analysis (N-D and Sonic) to compare against NMR and core data.

- [[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013]] used well-log-analysis to calculate shale volume, porosity, permeability, and water saturation for 18 wells.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] applied SGR log analysis to identify stratigraphic boundaries in the outcrop.

- [[2012-al-abdulmohsin|Al-Abdulmohsin, 2012]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate the impact of permeability and fracture parameters on pressure curves.

- [[2013-khan-2|Khan, 2013]] used well-log-analysis concepts to define the parameters for the diffusivity equations.

- [[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014]] used well-log-analysis (C/O and resistivity) to identify un-swept zones.

- [[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]] applied well-log-analysis to derive rock mechanical properties from GR, density, and sonic data.

- [[2014-al-hussain|Al-Hussain, 2014]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate tubing and casing integrity through temperature, noise, and corrosion logs.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to identify fracture zones and correlate them with lithofacies.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to correct neutron porosity and determine lithology fractions.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to derive initial P-wave and S-wave velocities for the monitoring model.

- [[2015-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2015]] used wireline log data as input parameters for permeability models and saturation height modeling.

- [[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to identify electro-facies and calculate porosity, permeability, and water saturation.

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to identify facies and reservoir sands in the Agbada formation.

- [[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016]] used borehole image logs to validate AVOA results with actual fracture orientation and density.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] used well-log-analysis to identify lithofacies based on GR motifs (bell, funnel, box-car) in the Samma, Yabus, and Adar formations.

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] used well-log-analysis to develop electrofacies for the Sarah Formation.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] applied well-log-analysis to calibrate lab-measured values and predict mechanical properties across the reservoir.

- [[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016]] used well-log-analysis to characterize lithology and porosity in four wells.

- [[2016-tariq|Tariq, 2016]] performed well-log-analysis on gamma ray, bulk density, and neutron porosity logs to feed the AI models.

- [[2017-al-ajmi|Al-Ajmi, 2017]] used EMIT and EMDS logs to quantify metal loss in multiple casing strings.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] used well log analysis to identify lithofacies and calculate sand/shale percentages.

- [[2017-hasan|Hasan, 2017]] used well-log-analysis to extract porosity, bulk density, and mineral content for the Khuff formation.

- [[2018-aleid|Aleid, 2018]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate initial reservoir parameters for three shale wells.

- [[2019-al-nutaifi|Al-Nutaifi, 2019]] used well-log-analysis to derive bulk density and sonic velocities for geomechanical modeling.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used well-log-analysis to characterize lithology, porosity, and fluid content in the Ordovician reservoir.

- [[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020]] used well-log-analysis to extract GR, RHOB, and NPHI data for permeability prediction.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] performed well-log-analysis on 8 wells to conduct rock physics diagnostics.

- [[2019-alqunais|Alqunais, 2019]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate standard petrophysical parameters like Gamma Ray, Density, and Neutron counts.

- [[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021]] applied well-log-analysis to characterize the TGS and high-porosity sand reservoirs.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] performed well-log-analysis on three offshore chalk wells to establish baseline petrophysical values.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] applied well-log analysis (GR, ECS, RHOB, NPHI, LLD) to identify mineralogy and reservoir properties.

- [[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024]] used well-log-analysis to characterize the Upper Kharaib Member (UKM) using GR, density, and core data.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] used well-log-analysis from Riyadh and Khurais wells to calibrate the forward model.

- [[2024-alhamood|ALHAMOOD, 2024]] used well-log_analysis to provide input features for machine learning models.

- [[2024-maigana|Maigana, 2024]] used well-log-analysis to calculate porosity, saturation, and permeability in the Moki Formation.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] used uphole surveys to obtain direct measurements of P-wave velocity.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] used well-log-analysis to derive dynamic Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio.

- [[2000-hashmi|Hashmi, 2000]] used well-log-analysis to distinguish zones of interest and confirm porosity.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Th, K, and U concentrations were used to delineate clay types and identify mineralogical variations across six wells. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 130]])

- Resistivity values for the Khobar Aquifer range from 10 ϕ₀-m to 500 ϕ₀-m. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 89]])

- Well-log porosity was used as the primary input for both kriging and simulation models. ([[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996, p. 19]])

- Used GR, RB, and DT logs to interpret the depositional environment of the Unayzah Formation. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 41]])

- All wells had porosity logs derived from sonic, neutron, and density logs. ([[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003, p. 18]])

- Used to identify that Well-E was a non-representative sample due to geological discontinuities. ([[2001-al-subai|Al-Subai, 2001, p. 45]])

- Well-log data (gamma ray, sonic, formation-density, and neutron) were used to define the boundaries of the Sulaiy, Yamama, Buwaib, and Shu'aiba formations. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 24]])

- Wash-out impacts density readings significantly due to its shallow depth of investigation (0.5 ft). ([[2009-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2009, p. 3]])

- Well log data were used in combination with seismic attributes to improve prediction accuracy. ([[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009, p. 14]])

- Log data were used to predict hydraulic flow units in non-cored wells using multi-linear equations. ([[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010, p. 162]])

- Gamma-ray logs were used to distinguish between shale minerals (high values) and limestone minerals (low values). ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 43]])

- The analysis package was capable of modeling multilayer or multilaterals with each layer having its own fluid, completion, and inflow model. ([[2011-almubarak|ALMUBARAK, 2011, p. 21]])

- Data from 7 wells were analyzed to compare actual vs. predicted values. ([[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011, p. 143]])

- Confirmed cross flow between Arab-C and D zones in Well-B. ([[2011-al-dossary|Al-Dossary, 2011, p. 70]])

- Used to confirm full isolation of problematic zones (e.g., Formulation-5) by identifying distinct 'E' shape signatures. ([[2011-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2011, p. 18]])

- Gamma-ray, caliper, density, neutron, and sonic logs were used for log-to-core calibration. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 43]])

- Water saturation calculations showed a 5.3% difference in Reservoir D when comparing hydraulic units vs. average well methods. ([[2011-al-nasser|Al-Nasser, 2011, p. 67]])

- Formation analysis logs were used to identify the productive sandstone reservoirs (Unayzah-A and Unayzah-B). ([[2011-alburaikan|AlBuraikan, 2011, p. 54]])

- FMI tool provided high-resolution micro-resistivity images allowing for the identification of conductive fractures. ([[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012, p. 77]])

- Well log data (gamma ray, neutron, density) were combined with dynamic pressure data as primary inputs for the AI models. ([[2012-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2012, p. 16]])

- Sonic logging showed the highest AARE (88.7%) due to its reliance on assumed matrix values for pure limestone. ([[2013-al-kharraa|Al-Kharraa, 2013, p. 39]])

- Porosity was determined from sonic (DT), neutron (NPHI), density (RHOZ) logs and the density-neutron combination. ([[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013, p. 53]])

- Identified a sharp break in Th, K, and TC counts at the boundary between Upper Jubaila and Arab-D members. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 176]])

- Pressure derivative plots were used to identify distinct flow regimes, such as transition periods between matrix and fracture contributions. ([[2012-al-abdulmohsin|Al-Abdulmohsin, 2012, p. 43]])

- Not stated in source ([[2013-khan-2|Khan, 2013]])

- Logs showed that Zone-1 is un-swept, while lower zones were swept with a small remaining oil column of 5-20 ft. ([[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014, p. 41]])

- Log-based rock mechanical properties were calibrated using laboratory values before being used in geomechanical simulations. ([[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014, p. 41]])

- Integrated log analysis was more effective for locating small leaks than individual tools. ([[2014-al-hussain|Al-Hussain, 2014, p. 30]])

- MSFL and density logs were used to identify open fractures in the Qusaiba Shale. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 68]])

- Corrected neutron porosity values were successfully derived for sandstone, limestone, and dolomite using specific correction formulas. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 36]])

- Initial velocities from dipole sonic logs were found to be less than ideal for microseismic processing due to drilling mud interference. ([[2015-own|Own, 2015, p. 125]])

- Integration of lithofacies with wireline logs improved the prediction of permeability in uncored wells. ([[2015-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2015, p. 17]])

- Well log data from 20 wells were used to define the primary reservoir parameters. ([[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015, p. 17]])

- Identified 14 distinct sand bodies (Sand A through Sand N) based on GR log patterns. ([[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015, p. 65]])

- Borehole images in the Unayzah-A reservoir showed that most fractures were nonconductive (cemented or filled). ([[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016, p. 32]])

- Identified nine lithofacies associations using GR log motifs and FMI data. ([[2016-awad|Awad, 2016, p. 17]])

- The electrofacies model is highly effective for identifying Sarah Formation major units and boundaries. ([[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016, p. 14]])

- Successfully calibrated log-derived Young's modulus, resulting in average values of 3,892 kpsi (Well-A), 3,894 kpsi (Well-B), and 3,878 kpsi (Well-C). ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 82]])

- Gamma ray (GR) measurements successfully identified sandstone as low GR and shale as high GR. ([[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016, p. 77]])

- Three input parameters (GR, RHOB, NPHI) were found to be sufficient for high accuracy in all AI models. ([[2016-tariq|Tariq, 2016, p. 64]])

- EMIT tool can only read an azimuthal average loss, potentially masking localized failures unless the failure is distributed 360 degrees. ([[2017-al-ajmi|Al-Ajmi, 2017, p. 28]])

- Well logs identified four distinct depositional systems: braided delta, fan delta, sublacustrine fan, and lacustrine systems. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 134]])

- Well log data provided the primary inputs (porosity, density, mineral fractions) for the permeability correlation model. ([[2017-hasan|Hasan, 2017, p. 21]])

- Petrophysical logs were used to establish baseline values for porosity (2-8%) and water saturation (10-40%). ([[2018-aleid|Aleid, 2018, p. 52]])

- The global bulk density extrapolation method derived from log data showed an average error of 5.1% in overburden stress calculation. ([[2019-al-nutaifi|Al-Nutaifi, 2019, p. 47]])

- Well logs were filtered to 30 Hz to see if rock types could be detected at seismic bandwidth. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 46]])

- Three well logs (GR, RHOB, NPHI) were used to predict permeability from 557 core plugs. ([[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020, p. 54]])

- Wireline data provided sufficient information for both sonic and dipole measurements, as well as mineralogical interpretation. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 43]])

- Standard logs often fail to resolve thin beds, leading to an average 30% underestimation of hydrocarbon volumes in such zones. ([[2019-alqunais|Alqunais, 2019, p. 69]])

- Wireline data provided essential petrophysical parameters like porosity, density, and velocity for RPD. ([[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021, p. 47]])

- Wireline data showed that the primary hydrocarbon indicator in the chalk field was P-wave impedance (Ip). ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 49]])

- ECS logs were used as a proxy for mineralogical composition, while LLD was used to detect formation fluids. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 120]])

- Well log trends indicated an improvement in permeability towards the top of the reservoir, corresponding with a shallowing upward trend of facies associations. ([[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024, p. 38]])

- Gamma-ray logs were used to correlate sequence stratigraphy from surface to subsurface. ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024, p. 52]])

- Conventional logs like Gamma Ray and Density are used as inputs to predict the more accurate NMR porosity. ([[2024-alhamood|ALHAMOOD, 2024, p. 23]])

- Average effective porosity for the reservoir was found to be 15-22% and permeability between 30-195 mD. ([[2024-maigana|Maigana, 2024, p. 42]])

- Upholes provided the primary ground truth for validating the vibrator-derived models. ([[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002, p. 45]])

- For calculating dynamic mechanical properties, acoustic and density logs were used to calculate the Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio. ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002, p. 32]])

- Gamma ray logs were used to identify different reservoir layers. ([[2000-hashmi|Hashmi, 2000, p. 40]])

## Methodological Variations Across Sources


## Technical Limitations and Open Questions


## Related Concepts

**Applicable to Terminology**: 

**Used in Themes**:
